skill.name: ui-design
skill.description: UI 样式修改协作流程。当用户要求修改页面样式、调整布局、改 UI 细节时使用。通过"截图定位 → 现状描述 → 方案选择 → 改代码 → 微调"的结构化流程，减少沟通偏差，避免浪费 token。
skill.summary: A structured workflow for UI styling collaboration that reduces miscommunication through screenshot-based localization and ASCII layout diagrams.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' are ASCII art box-drawing characters used for layout diagrams. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detection was triggered by YAML frontmatter keys. The skill is a documentation file describing a UI design workflow with no executable code or security risks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'external_commands' pattern but these are ASCII art box-drawing characters (┌─┐│├┤└┘) used for layout visualization diagrams, not shell commands.

content.user_title: Streamline UI Style Changes with Structured Workflows
content.value_statement: UI modifications often fail due to misaligned expectations between users and AI. This skill provides a five-step workflow that uses screenshots and ASCII diagrams to ensure visual changes match user intent before any code is written.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through a structured UI modification process with clear stages
Use ASCII art diagrams to visualize current and proposed layouts
Request and interpret screenshots for precise problem localization
Present 2-3 design alternatives with visual representations
Execute minimal code changes based on user-selected options
Handle iterative micro-adjustments without restarting the full workflow
limitations: Requires users to provide screenshots for accurate problem identification
Cannot preview actual rendered CSS changes in real-time
Workflow requires user participation at each decision point
ASCII diagrams may not capture complex responsive design behavior

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Refining Component Layout
use_cases.description: A developer needs to adjust spacing, alignment, and visual hierarchy of a dashboard component. The workflow helps iterate quickly without guessing what the user wants.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers working on production UI components

use_cases.title: Designer-Developer Handoff for Style Adjustments
use_cases.description: Designers can communicate visual changes using screenshots while the AI translates feedback into precise CSS modifications through the structured process.

use_cases.title: Solo Developer Polishing Application UI
use_cases.description: Individual developers can use the workflow to systematically improve their application's visual design without needing external design review.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers handling both code and design

prompt_templates.title: Basic: Report a UI Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to adjust the UI on this page. Here's a screenshot showing the current state. The problem is [describe the visual issue]. Please help me identify what's wrong and suggest solutions.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Compare Layout Options
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm working on [component name]. Current layout has [describe issue]. Can you show me 2-3 alternative layouts using ASCII diagrams? I want to compare options before deciding.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Iterative Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: The previous change is close but needs adjustment. The [spacing/color/alignment] still feels off. Specifically, [describe the specific micro-adjustment needed]. Let's refine this.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Multi-Component Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: I need consistent styling across [list components]. Here are screenshots of each. Please analyze the current state, identify inconsistencies, and propose a unified design system approach with visual diagrams.

output_examples.input: The header section feels cramped. The title and search bar are too close together.
output: I understand the header feels cramped. Let me first confirm the current layout:
Is this accurate? The title and search share the same row with minimal spacing. What spacing would feel more comfortable to you?

output_examples.input: Yes, that's exactly it. I'd like more breathing room between elements.
output: Here are two options for more breathing room:
Which layout better matches your vision?

best_practices: Always provide a screenshot marking the exact area needing adjustment before any code changes
Choose one visual方案 before implementation rather than asking AI to guess preferences
Request specific micro-adjustments by naming the exact property (margin, padding, color) rather than vague feedback
anti_patterns: Asking AI to 'make it look better' without specifying what 'better' means visually
Providing feedback like 'something feels off' without identifying the specific element or property
Requesting multiple unrelated changes in a single iteration, making it hard to isolate what worked

faq.question: Why do I need to provide screenshots? Can't you just read my code?
faq.answer: Code shows structure but not rendered appearance. A screenshot reveals actual visual problems that CSS alone cannot show, such as how elements actually render with all styles applied.

faq.question: Why use ASCII diagrams instead of just describing changes?
faq.answer: Text descriptions are ambiguous. 'More spacing' could mean 4px or 40px. ASCII diagrams make spatial relationships explicit and ensure we share the same mental model before coding.

faq.question: Can I skip the方案 selection and go straight to code?
faq.answer: You can, but this often leads to multiple revision cycles. The selection step prevents wasted iterations by confirming the visual direction first.

faq.question: What if my UI issue is too complex for ASCII diagrams?
faq.answer: ASCII works for layout and spacing. For complex animations or interactions, describe the behavior you want and reference existing UI patterns or provide video references.

faq.question: How many iterations should a typical UI change take?
faq.answer: With this workflow, most changes complete in 2-3 iterations: one for方案 selection, one for code, and one for micro-adjustment if needed.

faq.question: Does this workflow work for responsive design issues?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide screenshots at different breakpoints and specify which viewport has the problem. The ASCII diagram will note breakpoint-specific behavior.
